content:
# Numbers Chapter 29 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 183–185; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0136–pa_0137 | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_029_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_029_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_029_page_003.jpg`

---

### The Feast of Trumpets (vv. 1-6)

**29:1** And in the seventh month, on the first day of the month, a holy convocation shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do. A day of blowing it shall be for you.

**29:2** And you shall present a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to YHWH: one young bull, son of the herd, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:3** and their grain offering: fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for the bull, two-tenths for the ram,

**29:4** and one-tenth for each lamb, for the seven lambs;

**29:5** and one male goat for a sin offering, to make atonement for you;

**29:6** apart from the burnt offering of the month and its grain offering, and the continual burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings, according to their ordinance, for a pleasing aroma, a fire-offering to YHWH.

---

### The Day of Atonement (vv. 7-11)

**29:7** And on the tenth day of this seventh month, a holy convocation shall be for you, and you shall afflict your souls; any work you shall not do.

**29:8** And you shall present a burnt offering to YHWH, a pleasing aroma: one young bull, son of the herd, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year; unblemished they shall be for you.

**29:9** And their grain offering: fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for the bull, two-tenths for the one ram,

**29:10** a tenth, a tenth of an ephah for each lamb, for the seven lambs;

**29:11** one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the sin offering of atonement, and the continual burnt offering, and its grain offering, and their drink offerings.

---

### The Feast of Sukkot -- Day One (vv. 12-16)

**29:12** And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, a holy convocation shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do. And you shall celebrate a feast to YHWH seven days.

**29:13** And you shall present a burnt offering, a fire-offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH: thirteen young bulls, sons of the herd, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year; unblemished they shall be.

**29:14** And their grain offering: fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls, two-tenths for each of the two rams,

**29:15** and a tenth, a tenth of an ephah for each lamb, for the fourteen lambs;

**29:16** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.

---

### Day Two of Sukkot (vv. 17-19)

**29:17** And on the second day: twelve young bulls, sons of the herd, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:18** and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, by their number, according to the ordinance;

**29:19** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings.

---

### Day Three of Sukkot (vv. 20-22)

**29:20** And on the third day: eleven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:21** and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, by their number, according to the ordinance;

**29:22** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.

---

### Day Four of Sukkot (vv. 23-25)

**29:23** And on the fourth day: ten bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:24** their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, by their number, according to the ordinance;

**29:25** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.

---

### Day Five of Sukkot (vv. 26-28)

**29:26** And on the fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:27** and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, by their number, according to the ordinance;

**29:28** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.

---

### Day Six of Sukkot (vv. 29-31)

**29:29** And on the sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:30** and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, by their number, according to the ordinance;

**29:31** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offerings.

---

### Day Seven of Sukkot (vv. 32-34)

**29:32** And on the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:33** and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, by their number, according to the ordinance;

**29:34** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.

---

### The Eighth Day -- Solemn Assembly (vv. 35-40)

**29:35** On the eighth day, an assembly shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do.

**29:36** And you shall present a burnt offering, a fire-offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH: one bull, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;

**29:37** their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bull, for the ram, and for the lambs, by their number, according to the ordinance;

**29:38** and one male goat for a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.

**29:39** These you shall offer to YHWH at your appointed times, apart from your vows and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings and for your grain offerings and for your drink offerings and for your peace offerings."

**29:40** And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**General:** Numbers 29 completes the festival sacrificial calendar begun in chapter 28, prescribing offerings for three major occasions in the seventh month (Tishrei): the Day of Blowing (Yom Teruah, vv. 1-6), the Day of Atonement (Yom Kippur, vv. 7-11), and the Feast of Sukkot with its eighth-day conclusion (vv. 12-38), followed by a summary formula (vv. 39-40). The chapter is characterized by its highly formulaic repetitive structure, especially in the Sukkot section (vv. 13-34), which features a distinctive declining bull sequence: thirteen, twelve, eleven, ten, nine, eight, seven -- totaling seventy bulls over the seven days. This repetition is liturgical architecture, not redundancy, and has been preserved without condensation.

**Manuscript verification:** The Leningrad Codex pages (183-185) were examined and confirm the standard MT text throughout. The declining bull numbers are clearly legible across the three manuscript pages. The Vaticanus pages (pa_0136, pa_0137) align closely with the MT; no significant textual variants affect the translation. The Vaticanus renders *atzeret* (v. 35) as *exodion* ("concluding feast") rather than the more common *episynagoge*, an interpretive tradition noted but not followed in the consensus.

**Established conventions applied throughout:** (1) "Sons of a year" for *bnei shanah*; (2) "Pleasing aroma" for *re'ach nikhoach*; (3) "Continual" for *tamid*; (4) "Sin offering" for *khattat*; (5) "Holy convocation" for *miqra qodesh*; (6) "Any work of service" for *melekhet avodah*; (7) "Son(s) of the herd" for *bnei baqar*; (8) "Present" for the hiphil of *qarav*; (9) "Apart from" for *mil-bad*. These conventions were established in Numbers 28 and are maintained consistently here.

-- SOPHIA
